channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Morarjee Textiles Sets March 10 Date for 35th Creditors Meet Amid CIRP
item.description: Morarjee Textiles Limited will hold its 35th Committee of Creditors (COC) meeting on March 10, 2026. This procedural gathering is part of the company's Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP) initiated in February 2024. The company is operating under the supervision of Resolution Professional Mr. Ravi Sethia, with decisions on its future resting with the COC.

item.title: Paradeep Parivahan gets BSE nod for Rs 16 Cr preferential warrant issue.
item.description: Paradeep Parivahan Limited has secured in-principle approval from BSE Limited for a preferential issue of 10,40,000 convertible warrants. The issue, priced at Rs 156.46 per share, aims to raise approximately Rs 16.27 crore from promoters and non-promoters via private placement. This move signals a potential capital infusion to bolster the company's growth prospects.

item.title: Thomas Cook India, SOTC Partner Booking.com for Corporate Travel Solutions
item.description: Thomas Cook (India) and SOTC Travel have announced a significant strategic partnership with Booking.com to enhance accommodation solutions for Indian corporate clients. This collaboration integrates Booking.com's vast inventory of over 31 million listings into their corporate booking tools, promising advanced controls, transparent pricing, and India-specific servicing. The move aims to provide businesses with unparalleled choice and streamlined booking experiences.

item.title: SAR Televenture Launches ₹48.59 Cr Open Offer for Grand Foundry at ₹20
item.description: SAR Televenture Limited has initiated an open offer to acquire up to 24,29,550 equity shares of Grand Foundry Limited at ₹20.00 per share. The total offer value stands at ₹48.59 crore. This move aims to secure a majority shareholding in Grand Foundry, a move governed by SEBI (Substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011. The offer closes on April 9, 2026.

item.title: Yasho Industries Rating Affirmed IND BBB+; Outlook Positive
item.description: India Ratings & Research has affirmed Yasho Industries Limited's bank loan facilities rating at 'IND BBB+' with a 'Positive' outlook, resolving the 'Rating Watch with Negative Implications'. The affirmation signals adequate capacity to meet financial commitments, while the positive outlook suggests potential for future credit profile improvement.

item.title: India Defense Stocks Face Supply Chain Snag Despite Geopolitical Boom
item.description: Middle East conflicts are fueling global defense spending, boosting India's defense sector through domestic orders and exports. Government support, like increased capital spending and the DAP 2026, is strong. However, critical component supply chain issues, especially reliance on Israel, and potential execution delays pose significant challenges. High valuations for some companies also require careful investor scrutiny.

item.title: Interarch Building Solutions Secures ₹44 Crore Domestic PEB Order
item.description: Interarch Building Solutions Limited has announced a new domestic order valued at approximately ₹44.00 crore plus tax. The contract involves the design, engineering, manufacturing, supply, and erection of pre-engineered steel building (PEB) systems, with a completion timeline of 7 months. This win reinforces the company's strong order pipeline and execution capabilities in the growing PEB sector.

item.title: Gold, Silver ETFs Gain on Geopolitics; Analysts Urge Caution
item.description: Gold and silver ETFs rose Tuesday, March 10, 2026, boosted by a weaker US dollar and geopolitical concerns. Some silver ETFs jumped up to 4%. Analysts advised short-term caution but maintained a bullish medium-term outlook amid global uncertainties.

item.title: Kovai Medical Seeks Shareholder Vote for Independent Director Reappointment
item.description: Kovai Medical Center and Hospital is seeking shareholder approval to reappoint two Independent Directors, Mr. A.P.Ammasaikutti and Dr. K.Kolandaswamy, for a second five-year term. The approval will be sought through a postal ballot via remote e-voting from March 13 to April 11, 2026. This move aims to ensure leadership continuity and maintain governance standards.

item.title: WSFx Global Pay Expands Maharashtra Footprint with New Hinjewadi, Thane Branches
item.description: WSFx Global Pay Limited is set to expand its physical presence with two new branch offices in Hinjewadi and Thane, Maharashtra, tentatively opening on April 1, 2026. This strategic move aims to broaden its operational footprint in the state and enhance customer accessibility for its foreign exchange and cross-border payment services.

item.title: Gold and Silver Rally as Oil Prices Crash; Economic Signals Mixed
item.description: On Tuesday, March 10, 2026, gold and silver prices rose in India, following global trends. MCX gold gained about 1%, and silver jumped nearly 4%. The rally was fueled by a sharp drop in global crude oil prices, easing inflation worries and improving market sentiment. However, mixed US economic signals, including a weaker labor market and the strong US dollar, create uncertainty for precious metals.

item.title: India Steel Rides Safeguard Duties to Strong Domestic Growth
item.description: India's steel companies are using government safeguard duties to lift domestic prices and boost profits, creating strong earnings prospects separate from global markets, especially China's massive oversupply. Prices and margins for hot-rolled coil (HRC) jumped significantly in early 2026. While India boosts production, China is cutting output and increasing exports, adding to global trade issues. Europe's steel sector faces weak demand and higher costs despite some support. This shows India's advantage in its protected market.

item.title: Sunita Tools inks US defence pact for 155mm shell tenders
item.description: Sunita Tools has inked a non-binding MOU with an unnamed US company to jointly bid for defence tenders, focusing on 155 M107 calibre shells. This strategic alliance aims to secure global market access and boost its defence sector growth by leveraging US expertise.

item.title: Oil Prices Surge on Mideast Conflict Fears, Driven by Supply Worries
item.description: Crude oil prices are soaring due to the escalating Middle East conflict, sparking fears of immediate supply cuts. Unlike the 2008 oil crisis, which was driven by strong global demand, today's prices are rising because of geopolitical risk. Brent crude briefly hit $120 per barrel. This surge impacts import-reliant countries like India and boosts the energy sector, despite some analysts forecasting lower prices by 2026.

item.title: Oil Soars Past $115 Amid Mideast Tensions; Experts Tip Bonds for Safety
item.description: Global markets face volatility as Middle East tensions push oil prices over $115 a barrel. Investors are seeking safety, with experts recommending high-quality bonds maturing in 2-3 years as a strategic move to preserve capital amid uncertainty.

item.title: Indian Retail Investors: Ditch the Noise, Build Real Wealth
item.description: The share of individual investors in Indian equities has surged, driven by digitization and financial awareness, reaching 18.8% by September 2025. However, the overwhelming 'product noise' from over 5,000 stocks, 1,000+ mutual funds, and new offerings distracts from core wealth creation. Investors are urged to shift focus from chasing returns to controllable factors: increasing contributions and extending investment horizons for sustained growth.

item.title: Rajputana Stainless IPO: Anchor Book Withdrawal Hits Demand Amid Mixed Investor Interest
item.description: Rajputana Stainless's ₹255 crore IPO is seeing a sharp split in investor interest. Qualified Institutional Buyers (QIBs) are showing strong demand, nearly filling their portion, while retail investors are largely holding back, with only 6% subscribed by day two. The IPO's appeal is further complicated by a withdrawn anchor book, despite a small grey market premium. Bidding closes March 11 for the stainless steel product manufacturer.

item.title: Kerala High Court Presses Digi Yatra on Data Security, Seeks Data Board Clarity
item.description: The Kerala High Court is questioning how Digi Yatra protects passenger data. Justices want the government to explain the status of the Data Protection Board and have asked the Digi Yatra Foundation about alleged security flaws under the new data protection law.

item.title: Power & Instrumentation Gujarat Allots 4.84 Lakh Shares Post Warrant Conversion
item.description: Power & Instrumentation (Guj.) Ltd. announced the board's approval for allotting 4,84,600 equity shares following the conversion of warrants. This move increases the company's paid-up equity capital from ₹19.49 crore to ₹19.97 crore. The allotment is part of a larger warrant issuance program from September 2024, aimed at bolstering the company's financial standing.

item.title: Oil at $90: SpiceJet Warns Asian Airlines Face Fare Hikes
item.description: SpiceJet Chairman Ajay Singh flagged $90/barrel crude oil as unsustainable, predicting higher airfares due to escalating Middle East conflict and rising jet fuel prices. The warning highlights significant financial strain on airlines, particularly those in Asia with less hedging, potentially leading to fare increases, network cuts, and impacting expansion plans.

item.title: Silver Soars 3% on Easing Mideast Fears, Weak Dollar
item.description: Silver prices in India jumped 3.09% to ₹276 per gram on March 10, 2026. The rally was sparked by a weaker dollar and easing Mideast conflict fears. Investors await US inflation data for the next direction.

item.title: Voltas Braces for Summer Demand Amid Profit Pressures
item.description: Voltas expects strong demand from an early, hot summer and El Niño, backed by an Axis Securities 'Buy' rating and Rs 1,628 target. However, recent Q3 FY26 results showed a steep profit fall and shrinking margins due to higher costs and competition. The stock trades at a high P/E with little recent movement and mixed analyst views.

item.title: Indian Tyre Makers Face Profit Squeeze as Costs Soar
item.description: India's tyre makers are facing higher expenses due to surging global crude oil and natural rubber prices. These key raw materials now make up a large part of their costs, pushing companies to consider raising prices. Although recent profits have grown, this constant rise in costs poses a significant risk to future earnings for the whole industry.

item.title: IIFL Finance Allots ₹500 Cr NCDs at 8.60% Coupon
item.description: IIFL Finance has approved the allotment of 50,000 Senior, Secured, Redeemable Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs) Series D36 worth ₹500 crore. These NCDs carry an annual coupon rate of 8.60% and mature on March 24, 2027. The move aims to bolster the company's funding strategy and capital structure. The NCDs are secured by a first-ranking charge over the company's current assets and are proposed for listing on the National Stock Exchange (NSE).

item.title: Divgi TorqTransfer Systems Reschedules Investor Meetings to March 13
item.description: Divgi TorqTransfer Systems Limited has postponed its scheduled Analyst and Institutional Investor Meetings by one day. The meetings, originally set for March 12, 2026, will now take place on March 13, 2026, citing unforeseen exigencies. This operational adjustment is aimed at ensuring smooth engagement with stakeholders.

item.title: Ramco Systems partners TASL for defence MRO software, eyes C130J fleet.
item.description: Ramco Systems has inked a partnership with Tata Advanced Systems Limited (TASL) to deploy its advanced Aviation MRO software at TASL's upcoming defence maintenance facility. This facility will service the Indian Air Force's C130J Super Hercules aircraft, marking a significant step in modernizing India's defence MRO ecosystem. Ramco's integrated platform aims to enhance operational efficiency and streamline complex maintenance processes.

item.title: Bansal Wire Wins GST Appeal, ₹92 Lakh Tax Liability Reduced to Nil
item.description: Bansal Wire Industries Limited has received a favourable GST appeal order for FY 2021-22, eliminating a tax liability of ₹92,90,112. The company confirmed there will be no demand, penalty, or interest, with no expected financial or operational impact. This resolution reinforces the company's compliance framework.

item.title: AU SFB Stock Tumbles as Valuation Concerns Overshadow Growth Forecasts
item.description: AU Small Finance Bank (AU SFB) is navigating a complex market environment. While brokerage reports project robust loan and earnings growth through FY28, driven by its transition to universal banking and improving operational metrics, the stock's recent sharp decline highlights investor concerns. High valuations relative to peers, increasing funding costs, and lingering asset quality risks in microfinance present significant headwinds that temper the optimistic outlook.

item.title: Uno Minda Clarifies Product Report; Revenue 'Insignificant'
item.description: Uno Minda Limited clarified a news report on its trumpet horn range, confirming it's an existing product with insignificant revenue, not a new development. This clarification aims to prevent market speculation and uphold SEBI disclosure norms.

item.title: OBCL Promoter Ravi Agrawal Acquires Shares, Stake Edges Up to 45.16%
item.description: Ravi Agrawal, MD and promoter of OBCL Limited, has acquired 4,197 equity shares for ₹2.34 lakh through an open market purchase. This minor transaction nudged his total shareholding from 45.14% to 45.16%, reflecting a marginal increase in promoter confidence. The company, a logistics player, saw this disclosure filed on March 10, 2026.

item.title: Sika Interplant Q3 Profit ₹9.5 Cr; Corrects Financial Discrepancy
item.description: Sika Interplant Systems Limited announced its unaudited financial results for the quarter and nine months ended December 31, 2025, posting a standalone net profit of ₹9.50 crore on revenue of ₹50.31 crore for the quarter. The company also confirmed the correction of a previously identified discrepancy in its financial filings, uploading a revised report. This move aims to ensure greater transparency for stakeholders.

item.title: India Homes Secures OTS with JCF ARC, Resolves Secured Debt
item.description: India Homes Limited has announced a significant One-Time Settlement (OTS) with J.C. Flowers Asset Reconstruction Private Limited (JCF ARC), sanctioned on March 9, 2026. This agreement covers all outstanding secured borrowings, positioning the company to clear its secured debt upon successful payment. The development marks a crucial step towards resolving past financial distress.

item.title: BNP Paribas Backs Zomato, Swiggy With Big Stock Targets
item.description: BNP Paribas sees significant upside for Zomato and Swiggy, setting targets for 83% and 62.5% gains. Despite investor worries about AI and valuation drops, BNP believes the companies' structural strengths offer resilience. However, Zomato's high P/E of 93.3 and Swiggy's continued losses highlight differing financial paths in India's fast-growing food delivery and quick commerce markets.

item.title: Kwality Pharma Completes Phase 1 Bioequivalence Program for 30+ Complex Drugs
item.description: Kwality Pharmaceuticals Ltd. (KPL) has successfully concluded Phase 1 of its extensive Bioequivalence (BE) program, covering over 30 complex drug molecules. This milestone, achieved with four global CRO partners, spans critical therapeutic areas like oncology and cardiology. The completion positions KPL to file dossiers for new drug studies in regulated and other international markets, significantly bolstering its global regulatory footprint.

item.title: HGS stock surges on UP broadband deal, but financial clouds linger
item.description: Hinduja Global Solutions' (HGS) broadband unit, OneOTT Intertainment Ltd. (OIL), has signed a key agreement with the Uttar Pradesh government for Project GANGA. The deal aims to bring wired broadband to over 2 million households and create 100,000 jobs. While this offers significant long-term growth in India's digital sector, HGS faces financial challenges including shrinking profit margins and a negative price-to-earnings ratio.

item.title: Eco Hotels Committee to Decide Second Call for Partly Paid Shares
item.description: Eco Hotels and Resorts Limited will convene a Rights Issue Committee meeting on March 14, 2026, to deliberate on issuing second call(s) for its outstanding 1.28 crore partly paid equity shares. This move is a continuation of its rights issue process, aimed at managing capital and supporting growth initiatives. The decision comes as the company navigates its operational expansion, with recent results showing revenue growth alongside widening net losses.

item.title: Tejas Networks Surges on NEC Partnership; Valuation Worries Linger
item.description: Tejas Networks shares jumped over 9% on March 10, 2026, bouncing back after a sharp fall. The recovery followed news of employee stock option grants and a partnership with NEC Corporation for 5G MIMO radios. However, the company's negative P/E ratio and recent losses contrast with strong sector growth and government support, casting doubt on its valuation sustainability. Rivals like Sterlite Technologies also have negative P/E ratios, while HFCL's is very high.

item.title: India Rations Gas to Businesses Amid Geopolitical Supply Fears
item.description: India is reallocating natural gas supplies due to geopolitical disruptions in West Asia, impacting shipments and straining commercial users like restaurants and transport. The government's policy prioritizes essentials such as domestic gas and fertilizers, cutting back supply to industries like petrochemicals and some power plants. Major energy companies are now adjusting to revised delivery schedules.

item.title: India Fertilizer Stocks Jump on Gas Order Amid Crisis
item.description: Fertilizer stocks, including FACT Ltd., RCF Ltd., and Chambal Fertilisers Ltd., jumped Tuesday after India's government issued the Natural Gas Regulation Order, 2026. The rules cap natural gas supply to fertilizer plants at 70% of their average use over six months, requiring it solely for fertilizer production. This offers some certainty amid global supply disruptions from Middle East tensions and Qatar's force majeure, which have reduced India's LNG imports. However, the 70% cap could restrict operations, and the ongoing global energy crisis risks future supply and prices, affecting profit margins and the large government subsidy.

item.title: IOB ₹502 Cr Tax Demand Issued; Bank Confident of No Impact
item.description: Indian Overseas Bank (IOB) has received a demand notice from the Income Tax department for ₹502.29 crore related to Assessment Year 2017-18. The public sector lender is challenging the notice and has expressed confidence that it will not materially impact its financial operations or standing.
